name: Toques Waterfalls Trail
description: Jog the Toques Waterfalls Trail, a difficult 3.4-mile route in Serra do Careón, featuring the Waterfall of the Rego das Covas and Molino do
region_intro: Jogging routes around A Fonsagrada traverse a mountainous landscape that forms a natural border with Asturias, offering numerous trails through forests and along river valleys. The region is characterized by its elevated terrain, which provides challenging ascents and descents suitable for varied running experiences. A Fonsagrada is also an integral part of the Camino Primitivo, featuring sections known for their natural beauty and varied landscapes. Best jogging routes around A Fonsagrada The most popular jogging route is Toques Waterfalls Trail, a 3.4 miles (5.5 km) trail that takes 52 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through a forested environment. Another top favourite among local runners is Birth of the Miño River - circular via Peña de Lagoa and Cruz de Meira, a moderate 5.6 miles (9.0 km) path. This route explores the source of the Miño River with varied terrain. Local runners also love the Seimeira de Vilagocende loop from A Fonsagrada, a 12.6 miles (20.2 km) trail leading through river valleys and past the impressive Seimeira de Vilagocende waterfall, often completed in about 2 hours 57 minutes. Jogging around A Fonsagrada is defined by mountainous terrain, dense forests, and river valleys, with routes that cater to both moderate and difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 100 runners have used komoot to explore A Fonsagrada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails in A Fonsagrada?
A: A Fonsagrada is characterized by its mountainous landscape, dense forests, and river valleys. You can expect routes with challenging ascents and descents, often traversing natural borders with Asturias. The region's role in the Camino Primitivo also means many trails offer varied and scenic experiences through this elevated terrain.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes available?
A: The running routes around A Fonsagrada are generally quite challenging due to the mountainous terrain. While there are no routes explicitly rated as 'easy' in this guide, you can find a moderate option like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991268927/birth-of-the-mino-river-circular-via-pena-de-lagoa-and-cruz-de-meira" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Birth of the Miño River - circular via Peña de Lagoa and Cruz de Meira&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a 5.6-mile path with varied terrain suitable for those with some running experience.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, A Fonsagrada offers several excellent circular running routes. For example, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991268927/birth-of-the-mino-river-circular-via-pena-de-lagoa-and-cruz-de-meira" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Birth of the Miño River - circular via Peña de Lagoa and Cruz de Meira&lt;/a&gt;, or take on the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31449653" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seimeira de Vilagocende loop from A Fonsagrada&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 12.6-mile trail.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by waterfalls?
A: Absolutely! The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31449653" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seimeira de Vilagocende loop from A Fonsagrada&lt;/a&gt; is a fantastic option. This 12.6-mile trail leads through river valleys and directly past the impressive Seimeira de Vilagocende waterfall, which boasts a 50-meter drop within a beautiful natural environment. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991266661/toques-waterfalls-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toques Waterfalls Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 3.4-mile route featuring significant elevation gain through a forested environment with waterfalls.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while jogging in A Fonsagrada?
A: Beyond the stunning landscapes, you can encounter several natural highlights. Many routes offer views of the mountainous terrain and dense forests. Specific attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5416282" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Miño River&lt;/a&gt;, which is featured on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991268927/birth-of-the-mino-river-circular-via-pena-de-lagoa-and-cruz-de-meira" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Birth of the Miño River - circular via Peña de Lagoa and Cruz de Meira&lt;/a&gt; route. You might also discover other waterfalls like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5583844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Salgueira Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5291173" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seimeira de Murias Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the running trails in A Fonsagrada suitable for families?
A: Given the predominantly mountainous and challenging terrain, most of the running routes in A Fonsagrada are better suited for experienced runners or families with older children who are accustomed to strenuous outdoor activities. There are no 'easy' routes listed, with the majority being rated as 'difficult'.

Q: Are there any scenic jogging trails with good viewpoints?
A: Yes, A Fonsagrada's mountainous landscape provides numerous opportunities for scenic jogs with panoramic views. The region's peaks offer unparalleled vistas, and sections of the Camino Primitivo are renowned for their natural beauty. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31449943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seimeira de Vilagocende – Santa Bárbara - Silvela loop from A Fonsagrada&lt;/a&gt;, a longer 20.9-mile trail, will take you through diverse landscapes with many scenic spots.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in A Fonsagrada?
A: A Fonsagrada is an excellent destination for outdoor activities due to its natural beauty. While specific recommendations for jogging seasons aren't provided, the region's diverse vegetation and mountainous terrain suggest that spring and autumn would offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can also be good, but be prepared for potentially warmer weather, especially on exposed sections. Winter jogging might be possible, but conditions could be more challenging due to cold or snow at higher elevations.

Q: How many jogging routes are available in this guide for A Fonsagrada?
A: This guide features 8 distinct jogging routes around A Fonsagrada, ranging in difficulty from moderate to difficult. These routes have been explored by over 100 runners using komoot.

Q: What is the longest running route in this guide?
A: The longest running route featured in this guide is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31449943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seimeira de Vilagocende – Santa Bárbara - Silvela loop from A Fonsagrada&lt;/a&gt;. This challenging trail covers approximately 20.9 miles (33.6 km) and involves significant elevation changes, typically taking around 4 hours and 36 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks along the jogging routes?
A: While the focus is on natural beauty, A Fonsagrada is a crucial stop on the Camino Primitivo, an ancient pilgrimage route. Jogging along sections of this path connects you to centuries of history. You might also find historical sites nearby, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4527080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montouto Hospital and Dolmen&lt;/a&gt;, which is a historical site and also a natural monument.

Q: What makes the Camino Primitivo sections near A Fonsagrada good for running?
A: The Camino Primitivo near A Fonsagrada is known for its natural beauty and varied mountainous landscapes. It features challenging ascents and descents, offering great running tracks and opportunities for downhill running, making it ideal for those seeking a strenuous and rewarding jogging experience amidst stunning scenery.

Q: What is the most challenging running route in the guide?
A: Many routes in A Fonsagrada are rated as 'difficult'. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31449943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seimeira de Vilagocende – Santa Bárbara - Silvela loop from A Fonsagrada&lt;/a&gt; stands out as one of the most challenging, covering 20.9 miles with over 1397 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31449653" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seimeira de Vilagocende loop from A Fonsagrada&lt;/a&gt;, a 12.6-mile trail with nearly 1000 meters of elevation gain.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of jogging the Toques Waterfalls Trail?
faqs.answer: The Toques Waterfalls Trail is rated as difficult for jogging. While the distance is moderate at approximately 5.5 km, the significant elevation gain of around 317 meters makes it a challenging route. Expect steep sections that will test your endurance.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: For an average jogger, completing the Toques Waterfalls Trail takes approximately 52 minutes. However, due to its difficult rating and elevation changes, your actual time may vary depending on your fitness level and how often you pause to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail is named for its beautiful cascades. You'll encounter two notable waterfalls: the Waterfall of the Rego das Covas and the Waterfall of Molino do Penallo. These are the primary highlights and offer picturesque spots to pause and appreciate nature.

faqs.question: Is the Toques Waterfalls Trail a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The Toques Waterfalls Trail is designed as a circular route, meaning it is a loop trail. This allows you to experience different scenery throughout your jog without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: Where is the trailhead located and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The Toques Waterfalls Trail, also known as "As Trapelas Circular from Silvacha," is located in the Serra do Careón region. Specific trailhead parking details are not provided, but typically trails in such areas have designated parking near the starting point in or near the closest village, Silvacha. We recommend checking local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Toques Waterfalls Trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails within natural parks like Serra do Careón allow dogs, but they are usually required to be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Please check specific regulations for Serra do Careón before your visit.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog the Toques Waterfalls Trail?
faqs.answer: Given it's a waterfalls trail, spring and autumn are often ideal. Spring offers lush greenery and strong water flow from seasonal rains, while autumn provides cooler temperatures and beautiful foliage. Summer can be warm, so an early morning or late afternoon jog is recommended. Winters might bring colder, wetter conditions, potentially making some sections slippery.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Serra do Careón park?
faqs.answer: Most natural parks in this region, including Serra do Careón, do not require specific permits for day hikes or jogs on established trails, nor do they typically charge an entrance fee. However, it's always wise to check the official website of Serra do Careón for any updated regulations or special requirements before your visit.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginner joggers?
faqs.answer: No, due to its difficult rating and significant elevation gain over a relatively short distance, the Toques Waterfalls Trail is generally not recommended for beginner joggers. It requires a good level of fitness and experience with trail running or steep ascents.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain should I expect while jogging this route?
faqs.answer: As a difficult trail with significant elevation changes, expect varied terrain. This likely includes natural paths, potentially rocky or root-strewn sections, and steep ascents and descents. The presence of waterfalls suggests you might encounter damp or muddy areas, especially after rain.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for jogging the Toques Waterfalls Trail?
faqs.answer: Given the difficult rating and varied terrain, wear appropriate trail running shoes with good grip. Dress in layers suitable for the weather, and consider bringing a lightweight waterproof jacket. Carry enough water, as there might not be reliable sources on the trail, and a small first-aid kit. A fully charged phone and a map or GPS device are also advisable.

waypoints.name: Waterfall of the Rego das Covas
